_v2.4.0_changes: 1) GBPAUD + GBPCAD permanently blocked — 13 combined trades, 0% WR, -545 pips, MTF-supervised losses. 2) European session start delayed 08:00 → 09:30 UTC — noise zone blocked. 08:00-09:30 now treated as ASIAN (threshold 999). 3) sessions block added (european_start_hour / european_start_minute).
_v2.3.0_changes: 1) Full MTF hierarchy: 1h+30m+4h+Daily+Weekly — all required except weekly (bonus only). Daily is decisive filter. 2) Gemini 2.5 Flash replaces FinBERT for USD reasoning. FinBERT kept as fallback. 3) mtf_settings + gemini_usd_gate blocks added.
_v2.3.1_changes: 1) Weekly MTF fix: was incorrectly blocking signals — now bonus-only, never blocks. 2) pandas FutureWarning: resample('4H') → resample('4h'), offset='8h'.
_sentiment_note: v2.4.0: Gemini 2.5 Flash for USD pairs (BULLISH/BEARISH/NEUTRAL). GBP pairs remain pure technical. FinBERT = automatic fallback if GEMINI_API_KEY not set.

sessions._note: v2.4.0: Delayed from 08:00 UTC to 09:30 UTC. The 08:00-09:30 window (London open noise zone) is treated as ASIAN session (threshold 999 = blocked). Data: European session 22.7% WR, -406 pips. Early window is primary source of losses — price discovery, EUR volatility, overnight EMA misalignment.

mtf_settings._note: v2.4.0 / v2.3.1-FIX: Weekly is bonus-only — never blocks signals. Was incorrectly blocking in v2.3.0. Daily is the decisive filter — would have blocked the March 23 2026 -318 pip day (GBP in 3-day macro selloff, system was firing GBP BUYs all day). 4h resampled from 1h with offset='8h' anchored to London open.

gemini_usd_gate._model_note: NOT gemini-2.0-flash (deprecated Feb 2026, retired March 3 2026). Use gemini-2.5-flash.
gemini_usd_gate._cost_note: No Google Search grounding ($35/1K). Pass NewsAPI headlines as text context. Free.
gemini_usd_gate._logic: BULLISH USD → allow BUY USD pairs, block SELL USD pairs. BEARISH → allow SELL, block BUY. NEUTRAL → block all USD signals. None (API down) → FinBERT fallback.

aggressive._note: v2.2.0: 62→55. MTF hierarchy is now the quality gate (replaces raw score threshold).

conservative._note: v2.2.0: 62→57. Slightly stricter than aggressive to keep conservative mode tighter.

tiers._note: A+ (≥80) = trades. A (72-79) = BLOCKED — 0% WR, -107 pips, enters on already-extended moves. B (55-71) = main active tier. C (<55) = BLOCKED, net negative. B_min_score lowered 62→55 in v2.2.0 — MTF filter is quality gate.

session_thresholds._note: v2.4.0: 08:00-09:30 UTC window now returns ASIAN in code (threshold 999). OVERLAP still blocked. LATE_US at 60 — thin volume.

pair_limits._note: v2.4.0: GBPAUD=0 (7t 0% WR -286p, MTF-supervised), GBPCAD=0 (6t 0% WR -259p, MTF-supervised). Both also in _PERMANENTLY_BLOCKED_PAIRS in code. EURUSD=0 (7t 0% WR -177p). EURGBP=0 (no edge). GBPUSD=5 BUY-only. GBPJPY=1.

GBPUSD.reason: GBPUSD SELL: 0/3 wins (-49.7p live). BUY: 39%+ WR. BoE hawkishness = structural GBP bid.

usd_pair_rules._note: v2.4.0: EURUSD and GBPAUD/GBPCAD removed — all in _PERMANENTLY_BLOCKED_PAIRS. These 6 USD pairs are US-session-only with Gemini gate.
usd_pair_rules._gate_note: v2.4.0: Gemini 2.5 Flash replaces FinBERT. FinBERT fallback at threshold 0.1 if Gemini unavailable.

sentiment._setup: Requires GitHub secrets: GEMINI_API_KEY (primary), HF_API_KEY (fallback), NEWSAPI_KEY, MARKETAUX_API_KEY.

economic_calendar._note: Forex Factory blackout. GBP events cover GBPJPY and GBPUSD. USD events cover all USD-restricted pairs.

live_price_validation._note: X-Rates via Browserless. Corrects yfinance stale/diverged prices before signal is written.

_research_notes.GBPAUD_blocked: 7 trades, 0% WR, -286 pips. Fired under live MTF supervision in v2.3.1. Structural issue: AUD is commodity-linked, choppier than JPY. Scoring model trained on GBP/JPY momentum — not calibrated to AUD dynamics. Full stop every time.
_research_notes.GBPCAD_blocked: 6 trades, 0% WR, -259 pips. Same structural issue as GBPAUD. CAD is oil-linked, commodity-driven chop. Both GBP crosses permanently blocked in v2.4.0.
_research_notes.european_session_delay: Data: European session (full 08:00-13:00) had 22.7% WR, -406 pips. ChatGPT analysis: 08:00-09:30 is price-discovery zone — EUR macro events dominate early London, overnight EMA structures misaligned by EUR open volatility. Fix: delay start to 09:30. 08:00-09:30 → ASIAN (blocked).
_research_notes.mtf_daily_filter: Would have blocked entire March 23 2026 session: 6 GBP BUY signals fired into a 3-day GBP macro selloff. Daily EMA structure was BEAR. 1h and 15m both showed bounces but the daily blocked them all. -318 pips prevented.
_research_notes.gemini_vs_finbert: FinBERT: sentiment ON = 23.1% WR, -69 pips (vs OFF = 29.4%, +14 pips). FinBERT reads words as positive/negative/neutral — cannot reason about context. 'Fed signals caution' = NEUTRAL to FinBERT but could mean +80 or -80 pips. Gemini 2.5 Flash reasons about what headlines mean for price direction — materially different capability.
_research_notes.core_edge: GBPJPY: 90 trades, 32.2% WR, +177 pips. GBPUSD: 23 trades, 39.1% WR, +84 pips. US session: 46 trades, 37% WR, +87 pips. These are the only net profitable segments. Everything else is drag.
_research_notes.breakeven_math: ATR stop 2.5x, ATR target 7.0x → R:R ≈ 2.8. Breakeven WR = 1/(1+R:R) ≈ 26.3%. GBPUSD at 39% and GBPJPY at 32% are both meaningfully above breakeven.
